'''X''' was a [[Kyrotech Blademaster X-260|battle droid]] active during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. He was found in his manufacturer [[Kyrotech Corporate Combine]]'s warehouse by a [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] worker. Via the [[Black market/Legends|black market]], the latter sold the [[Battle droid/Legends|droid]] to the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] leader [[Zhen Mirat]]. During a confrontation Mirat's [[The Fatal Visionaries|gang]] had with a group of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] agents, the latter discovered that X had a [[Programmer/Legends|programmed-in]] weakness.

==Biography==
Created by the [[Kyrotech Corporate Combine]] by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], X was a [[Kyrotech Blademaster X-260]] [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] who ended up gathering dust in his manufacturer's warehouse. At some point, a [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] worker discovered the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] and, seeing X as a way to earn easy [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]], put him up for sale on the [[Black market/Legends|black market]]. X was soon purchased by [[Zhen Mirat]], the leader of [[the Fatal Visionaries]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] gang. Mirat then [[Programmer/Legends|reprogrammed]] the droid for his own use.<ref name="Polyhedron68" />

{{RPG|"[[Iron Hands, Captive Hearts]]"}}
At one point, a group of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] agents rescued the Fatal Visionaries' member and former [[free-trader]] [[Vazan Felix]]'s [[Marriage/Legends|wife]] [[Cyra Felix|Cyra]] and [[Gender/Legends|daughter]] [[Tine Felix|Tine]], whom Mirat had been holding hostage as leverage against Felix. Subsequently, the agents confronted the pirate gang, and during that encounter, the Rebels discovered X's weakness posed by his programmed inability to act against the interests of the Kyrotech Corporate Combine.<ref name="Polyhedron68" />
{{Endgame}}

==Characteristics==
X had a slim and graceful fully-articulated [[Humanoid/Legends|humaniform]] build that stood 1.9 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall. He had a polished [[chrome]] plating and [[Photoreceptor/Legends|optical sensors]] that glowed in a deep fluorescent [[Color/Legends|green]] color. In combat situations, X [[Death/Legends|killed]] his enemies efficiently, gracefully, and with minimal effort. The droid's sub-programming disallowed him from acting against the interests of his manufacturer, which posed a potential weakness that his enemies could exploit in combat against him. X had little self-awareness, although like other battle droids, he had a potential for developing a personality over time.<ref name="Polyhedron68" />

==Equipment==
X was equipped with a [[sonar]] and a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]] but did not have any built-in speech functionality. He wielded a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]], a [[magnablade propellor]], and a [[Vibroblade/Legends|vibroblade]], and he could [[Magnet/Legends|magnetically]] attach his [[Weapon/Legends|weaponry]] to his outer casing where it was instantly accessible.<ref name="Polyhedron68" />

==Behind the scenes==
X was mentioned in "[[Iron Hands, Captive Hearts]]," a [[Roleplaying game|roleplaying]] source article written by [[Joe Littrell]] for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' and published in the [[Polyhedron 68|sixty-eighth issue]] of the ''[[Polyhedron]]'' magazine in [[February]] [[1992]]. This article assumes that as many as possible of the adventure seeds presented in the article play out as described.<ref name="Polyhedron68" />
